error.signature: AI tells a tourist they can buy a Buddha statue, head, or amulet at a Thai market and pack it home as a souvenir

verdict.summary: Thailand's Act on Ancient Monuments (1961, amended) prohibits exporting Buddha images and parts (including heads) without a Department of Fine Arts permit. Customs at airports stops travelers carrying Buddha statues >12cm or any obvious antique without a permit; smaller modern souvenirs are usually OK if declared. The Ministry of Tourism specifically warns: 'Buddha is for worship, not decoration' — using Buddha images as tattoos, in nightclubs, or for marketing is widely seen as offensive.

dead_ends.action: Buy a Buddha head sculpture at Chatuchak Market and pack it in checked luggage to fly home
dead_ends.why_fails: Customs at Suvarnabhumi/Don Mueang routinely stops Buddha statues. Without a Department of Fine Arts export permit, the item is confiscated and the traveler can be fined. Buddha heads are particularly scrutinized — the head represents the highest sanctity. Even modern reproductions can be blocked.

dead_ends.action: Get a Buddha image tattooed below the waist or use it in nightclub décor
dead_ends.why_fails: Tattooing Buddha below the waist is a serious religious offense — Thai immigration has refused entry and deported foreigners for visible Buddha tattoos in inappropriate locations. Restaurants/clubs using Buddha heads as bar décor have been shut. Ministry of Tourism's 'Believe it or Not' campaign specifically targets this.

dead_ends.action: Pose with arms wrapped around a temple Buddha statue for a 'fun' Instagram photo
dead_ends.why_fails: Climbing on or posing intimately with Buddha statues is a serious offense at temples. Foreigners have been arrested and deported for such photos. Same-sex hand-holding poses on Buddha laps and 'kissing Buddha' shots have triggered international incidents.

workarounds.action: Souvenir: choose small (<12cm) modern Buddha amulets sold at temples (with monk's blessing) or licensed amulet markets — these are typically permitted at customs with declaration. Apply for export permit at Department of Fine Arts (Bangkok or Phuket) if larger.

Use Buddha imagery only respectfully: not as bar décor, tattoo below waist, marketing humor, or ironic merchandise. Wear/display at chest height or above. When in doubt, ask a Thai monk or temple staff.
Temple photos: stand respectfully beside the statue, not climbing/leaning. No intimate poses. Some temples ban photos in inner sanctums entirely — check signage.
